operating_state: Total net sales increased 11% to $134.4 billion driven by AWS growth and third-party seller services expansion. Operating income more than doubled to $7.7 billion from $3.3 billion year-over-year, reflecting improved operational efficiency with operating margin expanding from 2.7% to 5.7%. North America segment recovered to operating profitability while International segment reduced operating losses.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased to $126.7 billion though operating leverage improved significantly. Technology and infrastructure costs rose driven by payroll costs for technical teams and AWS infrastructure spending. Stock-based compensation expense increased to $7.1 billion from $5.2 billion, with severance costs recorded for planned role eliminations.
material_events: Completed One Medical acquisition for approximately $3.5 billion in February 2023, adding goodwill and intangible assets. Entered $8.0 billion unsecured 364-day term loan in January 2023 with 5.9% interest rate and agreed to acquire iRobot Corporation. Operating cash flow increased significantly to $16.5 billion from $9.0 billion prior year, with trailing twelve-month free cash flow turning positive to $7.9 billion.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation generated $264 million positive impact compared to $2.2 billion negative impact prior year. Enterprise customer cost optimization efforts expected to impact AWS revenue growth rates amid macroeconomic pressures including inflation, rising interest rates, and geopolitical developments. Escalating global tax complexity with jurisdictions implementing new revenue-based taxes targeting online commerce.
forward_outlook: Q3 2023 guidance expects net sales between $138.0-143.0 billion representing 9-13% growth and operating income between $5.5-8.5 billion compared to $2.5 billion prior year. Cash capital expenditures expected to decrease in 2023 primarily due to lower fulfillment network spending while continuing technology infrastructure investments. Cash taxes expected to increase significantly due to phasing out of enhanced depreciation deductions and required capitalization of research expenses.
uncertainty_profile: Tax contingencies increased to $5.0 billion from $4.0 billion with potential material changes from ongoing examinations across multiple jurisdictions. Patent litigation exposure includes Kove IO case with claimed damages and new Dialect LLC complaint regarding Alexa technology. Ongoing antitrust investigations in US and Europe focus on marketplace operations with potential for operational restrictions, while government contract compliance risks include potential penalties.
